summary: The session discusses Randarts, or random artefacts, in the game Dungeon Crawl Stone Soup, detailing their occurrence, identification, properties, naming conventions, and strategic use. The user highlights the complexity and randomness of these items, emphasizing their potential power and drawbacks in gameplay.
facts: Randarts appear randomly starting on D:4 in Dungeon Crawl Stone Soup. A typical 3-rune game produces around 10 to 20 randarts without divine intervention. Three gods, Okawaru, Trog, and Xom, can gift randarts to their followers. Randart weapons always have a brand, while randart jewellery has a base type that provides auto inscription properties. Randart spellbooks do not have special properties but contain a random assortment of spells grouped by themes. Randarts have randomly generated names, which can sometimes be amusing or named after the player. Randarts can be categorized into six quality levels, ranging from "Nice to Uber" to "Bad." Most randarts are not particularly useful due to the randomness of their properties. Players are advised to discard randarts that are not currently useful or do not fit specific situations.
